subject: AI Agent Development
description: Marking guidelines for AI Agent coding challenges covering correctness, agent design, safety, code quality, and framework usage.
importantNote: AI agent exercises are assessed on functional correctness, sound architectural design, safety considerations, code quality, and effective use of frameworks. Agents must be tested with multiple inputs to verify robustness.

general: Functional Correctness: Does the agent produce correct, grounded output for the given task?
Architectural Design: Are the correct patterns used (ReAct, RAG, supervisor) for the problem?
Safety and Reliability: Are max_iterations set? Are tool inputs validated? Are failure modes handled?
Framework Usage: Are LangChain/LlamaIndex/LangGraph abstractions used correctly and idiomatically?
Code Quality: Clear naming, appropriate logging, Pydantic schemas for tool inputs, type hints used
Observability: Is verbose mode / LangSmith tracing enabled to allow inspection of agent behaviour?

partialCreditPolicy.description: AI Agent challenge partial credit principles

rules.condition: Agent runs but produces hallucinated output (not grounded in tools/retrieval)
rules.marksAwarded: Deduct 20-30% — grounding is fundamental to agent quality

rules.condition: No max_iterations or timeout — agent can loop indefinitely
rules.marksAwarded: Deduct 15% — safety valve is mandatory in production agents

rules.condition: Tool schemas have vague or missing descriptions
rules.marksAwarded: Deduct 10% — poor descriptions cause tool misuse

rules.condition: Tool inputs not validated (no Pydantic schema, no path validation etc.)
rules.marksAwarded: Deduct 15% — unvalidated tool inputs are a security risk

rules.condition: RAG index rebuilt on every run (not persisted)
rules.marksAwarded: Deduct 10% — inefficient and costly in production

rules.condition: LangGraph state mutated in-place instead of returned as dict
rules.marksAwarded: Deduct 10% — breaks LangGraph's immutable state model

rules.condition: Correct architecture but fails on edge cases (empty results, API errors)
rules.marksAwarded: Deduct 10-15% — robust error handling is expected at intermediate level

expectations: Correctly implements single-agent with tools using AgentExecutor or LangGraph
Builds functional RAG pipeline with chunking, embedding, retrieval, and grounded generation
Uses Pydantic schemas for tool inputs
Understands LCEL chain composition with | operator
Can debug agent with verbose=True and intermediate_steps
commonErrors: Missing agent_scratchpad in prompt template — agent has no memory of previous steps
Not returning tool results to the model — model fabricates outcomes
RAG answers not grounded — model ignores context and uses training knowledge
Using string concatenation instead of PromptTemplate for dynamic prompts
Not persisting vector index between runs

expectations: Builds multi-node LangGraph with conditional routing
Implements reflection/self-correction loops in agent workflow
Uses Command objects for dynamic node routing
Implements multi-agent supervisor with task delegation
Evaluates agent output quality with LLM-as-judge or RAGAS
Handles prompt injection and validates tool inputs for security
Uses checkpointing for persistent stateful agents
commonErrors: LangGraph state returned as mutated dict rather than partial updates
Supervisor routing to wrong worker due to vague worker descriptions in prompt
No stopping condition in reflection loop — infinite refinement
MCP server without path validation — directory traversal vulnerability
Thread IDs reused across different users — state bleeds between conversations

criteria.description: Both tools correctly defined with Pydantic schemas; safe calculator with ast; agent handles multi-step questions; proper prompt with agent_scratchpad; max_iterations set
Both tools functional; minor schema issues or missing safe eval; agent works for most inputs
One tool fully correct; agent runs but fails on multi-step problems or edge cases
Tools defined but without proper schemas or validation; agent produces some correct output
Minimal correct elements; agent does not run or produces incorrect output
No meaningful attempt or code does not run
Complete LangChain + LlamaIndex pipelines; index persisted; source attribution working; refuses out-of-scope questions; faithfulness evaluator functional and accurate
Both pipelines work; persistence implemented; one of source attribution or faithfulness evaluator has minor issues
One pipeline fully correct; second pipeline has issues; persistence may be missing
Basic RAG chain works for one framework; chunking/embedding correct; no persistence or attribution
Documents loaded and indexed; retrieval attempted but generation has significant issues
Only document loading or embedding implemented
No meaningful attempt
Full LangGraph with all four nodes; correct state with add reducer; conditional routing on quality_score; SqliteSaver checkpointing; Mermaid diagram generated; structured output in researcher
All nodes present and functional; routing mostly correct; checkpointing works; minor state management issues
Three of four nodes correct; state management has some issues; routing logic present but may not enforce quality threshold correctly
Basic two-node graph (researcher + writer) working; reflection loop missing or incorrect
StateGraph structure correct but nodes have significant logic errors
Minimal LangGraph code present but does not run
No meaningful attempt
MCP server with all three tools; path validation prevents traversal; LangChain agent connects via MultiServerMCPClient; agent can explore and read files; async correctly implemented
All three tools work; agent connects; path validation present but may have edge cases; async handling mostly correct
Two tools working; MCP server runs; agent connects but has limited functionality
MCP server runs with at least one tool; no path validation or async issues
MCP server structure correct but tools have significant errors
Basic server structure only; tools not functional
No meaningful attempt
Full supervisor pattern with all four nodes; Command-based routing; worker_outputs dict reducer; compiler node synthesises correctly; handles multi-step task delegation
Three workers and supervisor functional; routing mostly correct; Command usage correct; compilation present
Two workers + supervisor; basic routing works; some state management issues
Single worker with basic supervisor; multi-agent delegation not fully implemented
LangGraph structure present; supervisor node exists but routing logic is hardcoded or incorrect
Minimal multi-agent attempt; no functional routing
No meaningful attempt
